# Admin Dashboard (Next.js)

I have enough verified detail. Writing the section now.

## Admin Dashboard (Next.js Web) — Internal Control Center

The internal control center for `{{PLATFORM_NAME}}`. Separate Next.js (App Router) deployment from the hotel/property-manager dashboard, gated to `admin` and `super_admin` only. RTL-first (Arabic default), with `dir="rtl"` driven by the active locale and full LTR fallback for FR/EN.

### 0. Trust boundary & access model (the spine everything hangs off)

- **Two Supabase clients, never mixed:**
  - `createServerClient()` (anon key + user JWT from cookies, via `@supabase/ssr`) — runs as the logged-in admin, RLS applies. Used for all *reads* the admin is entitled to under policy.
  - `createServiceClient()` (service-role key) — lives in a `lib/supabase/admin.ts` file with `import 'server-only'` at the top so a client-component import is a build error. The service key is read from a non-`NEXT_PUBLIC_` env var (`SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`). It is **only** instantiated inside Server Actions, Route Handlers, and Edge Functions — never shipped to the browser, never in a `'use client'` module. (Per Supabase guidance: service-role bypasses RLS entirely and equals DB root; admin keys must never carry the `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix, and Server Actions "can be called by anyone" so every privileged action must re-check identity server-side.)
- **The cardinal rule for this subsystem:** *bypassing RLS is not the same as being authorized.* Every Server Action that uses the service client begins with the same gate, executed server-side, before any mutation:
  1. `getUser()` from the SSR client (validates the JWT against Supabase Auth, not just the cookie).
  2. Load the caller's role from `profiles.role` (or a `user_roles` table) using the **service client** (so an attacker can't hide their own row via RLS).
  3. Assert `role IN ('admin','super_admin')`; assert `super_admin` specifically for the sensitive actions flagged below.
  4. Only then run the privileged mutation, and write the audit-log row in the **same** action.
- This gate is factored into a wrapper, e.g. `withAdmin(action, { requireSuperAdmin })`, so it can't be forgotten per-action.
- **Why service-role and not "fix the RLS":** normal-user RLS policies are deliberately restrictive (a guest can only see their own bookings; a host only their own listings). Admin oversight needs cross-tenant reads/writes that *should not* be expressible as a normal-user policy. We model admin power as: (a) a small set of **read** RLS policies keyed on `is_admin()` for live dashboards/Realtime, and (b) all **destructive/sensitive writes** routed through service-role Server Actions/Edge Functions with explicit gating + audit. This keeps the browser bundle free of god-mode while still giving fast reads.

```sql
-- helper used by admin-read RLS policies (SECURITY DEFINER, search_path locked)
create or replace function public.is_admin() returns boolean
language sql stable security definer set search_path = public as $$
  select exists(select 1 from profiles
                where id = auth.uid() and role in ('admin','super_admin'));
$$;
-- example admin read policy
create policy "admin reads all bookings" on bookings
  for select to authenticated using ( public.is_admin() );
```

### 1. Route map & screen inventory

Route group `app/(admin)/` behind `middleware.ts` (redirects non-admin sessions to `/login`; the *authoritative* role check still happens in each Server Action — middleware is UX, not security).

| Route | Screen | Primary data source |
|---|---|---|
| `/admin` | **Overview / KPIs** — total bookings, GMV, commission revenue, active listings, new users, conversion rate; time-range filter (today / 7d / 30d / quarter / custom) + charts | materialized views (§5) via server component |
| `/admin/users` | **User management** list — search by name/phone/email, filter by role, status (`active`/`suspended`/`banned`), wilaya, verification state | `profiles` + `host_profiles` |
| `/admin/users/[id]` | **User profile** — KYC docs, verification toggle, suspend/ban, role chips, lifetime GMV, their bookings + listings + reviews tabs | joins across `bookings`, `properties`, `reviews` |
| `/admin/listings` | **Moderation queue** — default filter `status = 'pending'`, oldest-first; tabs: Pending / Reported / Suspended / All | `properties` |
| `/admin/listings/[id]` | **Listing review** — full property + room types + photos (Storage signed URLs), host info, approve / reject (reason) / request-changes / suspend | `properties`, `room_types`, `property_photos` |
| `/admin/bookings` | **Booking oversight** — filter by status, wilaya, date range, payment state, has-dispute | `bookings` + `transactions` |
| `/admin/bookings/[id]` | **Booking detail** — timeline, guest/host, payment + refund state, force-cancel / force-refund, linked dispute | `bookings`, `transactions`, `disputes` |
| `/admin/payments` | **Payments & transactions** — all `transactions`, provider (Chargily / Baridi), status, refunds; commission ledger | `transactions`, `commission_ledger` |
| `/admin/payouts` | **Payout management** — pending host payouts, approve/release/mark-paid, RIB/CCP details, batch view | `payouts` |
| `/admin/reviews` | **Reviews moderation** — reported & flagged reviews, remove (reason), restore | `reviews`, `reports` |
| `/admin/disputes` | **Disputes / support tickets** — guest↔host complaints, status workflow, internal notes, resolution | `disputes`, `dispute_messages` |
| `/admin/content/property-types` `/amenities` `/wilayas` `/communes` | **Master data** CRUD | `property_types`, `amenities`, `wilayas`, `communes` |
| `/admin/content/collections` | **Featured collections / home rails** — curate ordered property lists per rail | `featured_collections`, `collection_items` |
| `/admin/content/banners` | **Promo banners** — image (Storage), locale-keyed copy (ar/fr/en), schedule window, target link | `promo_banners` |
| `/admin/reports` | **Reports & analytics** — revenue, top destinations, host performance, growth cohorts; CSV/XLSX export | materialized views (§5) |
| `/admin/audit` | **Audit log viewer** — filterable by actor, action, entity, date; read-only | `audit_log` |
| `/admin/settings` | **Platform settings** (super_admin) — commission %, payout cadence, feature flags | `platform_settings` |

### 2. Data-fetching pattern in Next.js

- **Reads → React Server Components** using the SSR (anon+JWT) client under `is_admin()` RLS. Lists are paginated with `.range()` + keyset where possible; filters live in the URL search params so views are shareable/bookmarkable and cacheable.
- **KPI tiles & report tables** → server components querying materialized views (§5); wrapped in `<Suspense>` with skeletons, `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` (or `revalidate = 60` for the MV-backed dashboard since MVs refresh on a schedule anyway).
- **Mutations → Server Actions** (`'use server'`), each wrapped in `withAdmin(...)`, using the **service client**. After a successful write they `revalidatePath('/admin/...')`. No mutation logic ever lives in a client component; client components only render forms/confirm-dialogs and call the action.
- **Client components** are limited to interactive shells: filter bars, charts, confirm modals, the realtime queue badge. They receive data as props or subscribe to Realtime with the **anon** client (RLS-gated).
- **Heavy/long-running or third-party-touching operations** (issuing a Chargily refund, releasing a payout, fan-out notifications) run in **Supabase Edge Functions** invoked by the Server Action, not inline — keeps secrets (Chargily secret key, payout provider creds) in the Edge runtime and gives a clean retry boundary.
- **Live queue** (`/admin/listings`): Supabase Realtime subscription on `properties` filtered to `status=eq.pending` drives an unread-count badge and optimistic list insert; the row data itself is still RLS-checked.

### 3. Listing approval flow (end to end)

```
HOST (Expo, host mode)
  └─ creates Property  → status='draft'  (RLS: host can CRUD own drafts)
  └─ taps "Submit for review"
        → Server Action submitListing(propertyId)
           • validates required fields/photos/room types complete
           • status: 'draft' → 'pending', sets submitted_at
           • inserts notification to admins (type='listing_submitted')

ADMIN QUEUE  (/admin/listings?status=pending)
  └─ Realtime badge increments; row appears oldest-first
  └─ opens /admin/listings/[id], reviews photos + details

DECISION  (Server Action, service client, withAdmin)
  ├─ APPROVE  → moderateListing(id,'approve')
  │     status='pending' → 'approved'; sets approved_at, reviewed_by=auth.uid()
  │     property becomes visible to guests (guest-facing RLS: status='approved' AND is_published)
  │     audit_log + notification to host (push via Expo + in-app, FR/AR/EN copy)
  │
  ├─ REJECT   → moderateListing(id,'reject', {reason_code, reason_note})
  │     status='pending' → 'rejected'; stores reason_code (enum) + free-text note
  │     audit_log + notification to host with reason; host can edit → resubmit (back to 'pending')
  │
  ├─ REQUEST CHANGES → status → 'draft' with reviewer note (host fixes, resubmits)
  │
  └─ SUSPEND (a live listing) → moderateListing(id,'suspend',{reason})
        status='approved' → 'suspended'; immediately hidden from guests;
        active future bookings flagged for ops review; audit_log + host notice
```

- `listing_status` enum: `draft | pending | approved | rejected | suspended`.
- `rejection_reason` enum: `incomplete_info | poor_photo_quality | prohibited_content | duplicate | suspected_fraud | policy_violation | other` (drives localized host-facing copy + analytics on rejection causes).
- Notifications: one `notifications` row (in-app) + Expo push token fan-out, all done inside an Edge Function so a push-provider failure can't roll back the status change.

### 4. Destructive/sensitive actions — audit + permission matrix

Every privileged Server Action writes an `audit_log` row **in the same transaction-ish unit** as the mutation (insert audit row immediately after the write within the action; if the write is in an Edge Function, the function writes both). 

`audit_log` columns: `id, actor_id, actor_role, action (enum), entity_type, entity_id, before (jsonb), after (jsonb), reason_code, reason_note, ip, user_agent, created_at`. It is **append-only**: an RLS policy allows `is_admin()` to `SELECT`, **nobody** can `UPDATE`/`DELETE` (no policy grants it; even service-role writes go through a dedicated `log_audit()` `SECURITY DEFINER` function that only inserts).

| Action | Audit `action` | `before`/`after` captured | Min role | Extra confirmation |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Approve listing | `listing.approve` | status | admin | — |
| Reject listing | `listing.reject` | status + reason | admin | reason_code required |
| Suspend live listing | `listing.suspend` | status, is_published | admin | reason + typed property title to confirm |
| Verify host / KYC | `user.verify` | verification fields | admin | — |
| Suspend user | `user.suspend` | status | admin | reason required |
| **Ban user** | `user.ban` | status, role | **super_admin** | reason + typed email confirm |
| Force-cancel booking | `booking.force_cancel` | booking status | admin | reason + acknowledge guest/host impact |
| **Force-refund** (money out) | `booking.force_refund` | txn status, amount | **super_admin** | reason + amount re-entry; routes to refund Edge Function |
| Issue/adjust refund | `payment.refund` | txn status, amount | admin (full) / super_admin (>threshold) | amount re-entry |
| Release / mark payout paid | `payout.release` | payout status, amount | **super_admin** | reason + amount + RIB/CCP shown |
| Remove review | `review.remove` | review body, rating | admin | reason_code required |
| Edit master data (amenity/type/wilaya) | `content.update` | row diff | admin | — |
| Publish/schedule promo banner | `content.banner_publish` | banner fields | admin | — |
| Change commission % / payout cadence | `settings.update` | setting before/after | **super_admin** | typed confirm |
| Change another user's role / grant admin | `user.role_change` | old/new role | **super_admin** | typed email confirm |

Dangerous mutations also require a **fresh re-auth** for super_admin actions (re-enter password or step-up) when the session is older than N minutes — checked server-side in `withAdmin({ requireSuperAdmin, requireRecentAuth })`.

### 5. Charts / analytics & the queries behind the KPIs

- **Charting:** Recharts (composes cleanly in client components, light bundle). Charts are client components fed pre-aggregated rows from server components — the browser never runs aggregation over raw tables.
- **KPIs are backed by Postgres materialized views**, refreshed on a schedule via `pg_cron` (e.g. every 5 min) with `REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY`. This keeps the dashboard fast and avoids hammering `bookings`/`transactions` on every page load.

| MV | Grain | Feeds |
|---|---|---|
| `mv_daily_metrics` | per day: `bookings_count, gmv_dzd, commission_dzd, new_users, completed_bookings` | overview tiles + time-series charts |
| `mv_conversion_funnel` | per day: `listing_views, booking_starts, bookings_paid` → conversion % | conversion KPI |
| `mv_top_destinations` | per wilaya/commune: `bookings, gmv_dzd` (rolling 30/90d) | top-destinations bar chart, reports |
| `mv_host_performance` | per host: `listings_active, bookings, gmv_dzd, avg_rating, cancellation_rate, response_rate` | host performance table |
| `mv_revenue_by_period` | day/week/month rollups of GMV vs commission | revenue report + growth chart |

- **GMV vs commission distinction:** `gmv_dzd` = gross value of paid/confirmed bookings; `commission_dzd` = platform take per `platform_settings.commission_pct` recorded into `commission_ledger` at payment-capture time (not recomputed retroactively, so historical reports stay stable if the rate changes). All monetary columns are integer **DZD** (minor unit not used since DZD has no subdivision in practice for v1), rendered with Western-Arabic numerals + `دج`.
- **Time-range filter** maps to a `WHERE day BETWEEN ...` on the MV; "custom" range hits the MV unless granularity below a day is requested (rare for admin).
- **Exports** (`/admin/reports`): a Server Action queries the MV and streams CSV; XLSX via a library in a Route Handler (`/admin/reports/export?type=revenue&from=&to=`) returning a `Blob` with `Content-Disposition: attachment`. Export actions are themselves audited (`report.export`) since they egress PII/financials.

### 6. Payments & payouts oversight specifics (Chargily + Baridi)

- `transactions` columns include `provider (enum: chargily | baridi)`, `provider_ref` (Chargily `checkout.id` / Baridi reference), `status (enum: pending | paid | failed | canceled | refunded | partially_refunded)`, `amount_dzd`, `fees_dzd`, `booking_id`, `raw_payload (jsonb)`.
- **Status truth comes from webhooks, not the browser.** A Supabase Edge Function (`chargily-webhook`) is the Chargily `webhook_endpoint`; it verifies the `signature` header = HMAC-**SHA256** of the raw request body using the Chargily **secret key** (constant-time compare), then maps event `type` → transaction status:
  - `checkout.paid` → `paid` (+ confirm booking, write `commission_ledger`),
  - `checkout.failed` → `failed`,
  - `checkout.canceled` → `canceled`.
  Verified against the Chargily Pay v2 webhook docs (events `checkout.paid` / `checkout.failed` / `checkout.canceled`, header `signature`, HMAC-SHA256). Base URLs: `https://pay.chargily.net/test/api/v2/` (test) and `https://pay.chargily.net/api/v2/` (prod).
- **Refunds** initiated from `/admin/bookings/[id]` or `/admin/payments` call a `process-refund` Edge Function (holds provider creds), which updates the txn to `refunded`/`partially_refunded`, reverses the matching `commission_ledger` entry, and writes `payment.refund` audit. Baridi (BaridiMob QR) refunds that aren't programmatic are recorded as a **manual** refund txn with `provider='baridi'` and an uploaded proof reference, still audited.
- **Payout management:** `payouts` (`host_id, period, gross_dzd, commission_dzd, net_dzd, status: pending|approved|released|paid|failed, rib_ccp, proof_url`). Release is super_admin-only (§4), executed via Edge Function, audited as `payout.release`.

### 7. RTL / i18n for the dashboard

- `next-intl` with locales `ar | fr | en`; `ar` default. Root layout sets `<html lang={locale} dir={locale==='ar'?'rtl':'ltr'}>`. Tailwind uses **logical properties** (`ms-*/me-*/ps-*/pe-*`, `text-start/text-end`) so the entire admin UI mirrors for Arabic without per-component overrides. Charts: axis orientation and legend alignment flipped for RTL; numerals stay Western-Arabic (`1,234`) per spec, currency suffix `دج`.
- Master-data and moderation **reason strings, banner copy, notification templates** are stored locale-keyed (`{ ar, fr, en }` jsonb) so host-facing rejection notices and guest-facing content render in the recipient's language, independent of the admin's UI language.

---

**Sources relied on:**
- [Chargily Pay v2 — Webhooks](https://dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/webhooks) (event types, `signature` header, HMAC-SHA256 verification, payload fields)
- [Chargily Pay v2 — Create a checkout](https://dev.chargily.com/pay-v2/api-reference/checkouts/create) (request/response fields, `payment_method` values, `locale`, base URLs)
- [Supabase — Row Level Security](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/database/postgres/row-level-security) and [Why is my service-role client getting RLS errors](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/troubleshooting/why-is-my-service-role-key-client-getting-rls-errors-or-not-returning-data-7_1K9z) (service-role bypasses RLS; admin-read policy idiom)
- [Mastering Supabase Admin API Keys with Next.js](https://www.inksh.in/blog/next-tutorial/supabase-admin-server) and [Supabase service-role key — when to use it](https://securie.ai/guides/supabase-service-role-key) (`server-only` boundary, no `NEXT_PUBLIC_` prefix, re-check auth in Server Actions)
